## SpaceX / "SPCX" Stock Price Research — June 15, 2026

### Key Finding: "SPCX" Is NOT SpaceX

The ticker **SPCX** that trades on the NYSE Arca is the **SPAC and New Issue ETF** (formerly the Defiance Next Gen SPAC Derived ETF) — an exchange-traded fund focused on companies that went public via SPACs. **It has no connection to Elon Musk's SpaceX.** The name similarity is coincidental.

### SpaceX Public Trading Status

**As of the latest confirmed data (through early 2025), SpaceX has NOT gone public.** SpaceX remains a **privately held company** and does not trade on any public stock exchange under any ticker.

- Elon Musk has historically stated SpaceX would not IPO until the Starship program and Mars mission goals are more mature.
- **Starlink**, SpaceX's satellite internet subsidiary, has been discussed as a potential IPO candidate, but no confirmed filing had been made.
- I was unable to access live financial data sources to confirm whether anything changed between early 2025 and June 2026 (search tools were unavailable in this session).

### SpaceX Private Market Valuation (Last Confirmed)

- **Late 2024 valuation: ~$180–$210 billion**, making it one of the most valuable private companies globally.
- Shares trade on **secondary markets** (Forge Global, EquityZen) and through periodic internal **tender offers** to employees.

### Important Caveats

⚠️ **Data freshness limitation**: I was unable to access live web sources for today's date (June 15, 2026). It is possible that SpaceX or Starlink may have filed for or completed an IPO between early 2025 and now. Authoritative sources to check include:
- SEC EDGAR for S-1 filings
- Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC for IPO news
- Forge Global / EquityZen for secondary market pricing

As of early 2025, SpaceX had NOT gone public via IPO and did not trade on any public stock exchange.
SpaceX's private market valuation was approximately $180–$210 billion as of late 2024.
SpaceX shares trade on secondary markets (Forge Global, EquityZen) and through periodic internal tender offers.
Elon Musk has stated SpaceX would not IPO until Starship/Mars mission objectives are more mature.
Starlink (SpaceX subsidiary) has been discussed as a potential separate IPO candidate, but no S-1 filing had been confirmed as of early 2025.
